champion.narrative: Marcie Rhodd (Strategic Sourcing Leader) is the sole engaged contact and is positioned as an Evaluator, not yet a Champion. She has shown project ownership (drove the 18 Jun discovery call, asked detailed integration questions, agreed to NDA execution), but there is no evidence yet of advocacy (no internal introductions, no competitive deflection, no proactive agenda-pushing). She has access to the 2027 project and committee approval process but has not connected Zycus to the Economic Buyer, committee members, or any executive sponsor. Her engagement is active (last contact 18 Jun, 13 days ago; follow-up blocked by email/invite delivery issue, not disinterest), but the relationship is transactional, not champion-level. Developing her into a champion requires: (1) resolving the technical barrier, (2) executing the NDA and running the deeper discovery call with solutions consultant, (3) demonstrating integration wins (iRequest, Dynamics 365, software-broker punch-outs) to build her confidence in Zycus, and (4) asking her to introduce the Economic Buyer and committee members.

sources: Discovery call 18 Jun: Marcie mentioned Prevalent GRC/RFX tool rolling out now
Discovery call 18 Jun: Marcie stated they will not replace iRequest CLM; integration required
Known competitor (prior sweep): Prevalent, iRequest, do-nothing/manual process
competition.narrative: Two competitors confirmed: (1) Prevalent (GRC/RFX tool) is rolling out now at Desert Financial, creating sourcing-module overlap with Zycus iSource. If Prevalent satisfies the RFX need, urgency for a full S2P platform may decline and the committee may defer the 2027 project. Marcie mentioned Prevalent on the 18 Jun discovery call. (2) iRequest (CLM incumbent) is not a direct S2P competitor but is a retention risk: Marcie stated they will NOT replace iRequest, so Zycus must demonstrate seamless integration with iRequest CLM or risk being disqualified on the criteria. The wedge: position Zycus as the full source-to-pay platform (iSource + eProcurement + eInvoicing + Merlin AI intake/negotiation) that INTEGRATES with iRequest and Dynamics 365, vs Prevalent (GRC/RFX point solution) and manual processes. Emphasize Merlin AI as the differentiator for intake automation and supplier negotiation, and prove the iRequest/Dynamics integrations in the demo. If Prevalent is positioned as 'good enough' for sourcing, Zycus loses; the activation is to demonstrate that RFX alone (Prevalent) leaves procurement, invoicing, and AI intake gaps that only a full S2P platform solves.

identify_pain.narrative: Marcie Rhodd (Strategic Sourcing Leader) confirmed Desert Financial is evaluating a source-to-pay platform to replace manual procurement processes, with a 2027 project timeline and committee approval required (discovery call 18 Jun). The pain is operational: manual workflows, lack of integration between procurement, sourcing, and invoicing, and need for a unified S2P platform. Marcie specifically raised integration concerns (iRequest CLM incumbent must stay, Dynamics 365 ERP must integrate, software-license broker punch-outs required), signaling that the current stack is fragmented and the pain owner is Procurement/Sourcing (Marcie's domain). The pain is confirmed but not quantified; the financial/efficiency impact (cost, cycle time, error rate) has not been stated. Pain owner: Marcie Rhodd, Strategic Sourcing Leader.

competitive_position.summary: Competing against Prevalent (GRC/RFX tool, rolling out now) and iRequest (CLM incumbent, non-negotiable integration requirement). Prevalent is the direct threat to urgency: if it satisfies sourcing/RFX needs, committee may defer full S2P project into 2027 or beyond. iRequest is a compatibility gate: must demonstrate seamless integration or risk disqualification. Do-nothing (manual processes) is the third competitor if urgency collapses. Zycus is positioned as the full S2P platform with AI (Merlin intake/negotiation), but has not yet demonstrated integration wins or differentiated against Prevalent's RFX-only scope. Competitive positioning is early; no competitive review or head-to-head comparison has occurred.
